subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The Disaster Cycle
description: A fast breakdown of The Disaster Cycle for builders.

hook.text: Emergency management isn't about reacting to disasters. It's a four-phase cycle that never ends.

buildup.text: Mitigation reduces risk. Preparedness builds readiness. Response manages the crisis.

discovery.text: Recovery restores normalcy—and feeds back into mitigation for the next event.

twist.text: Most funding goes to response and recovery. Experts say mitigation saves $6 for every $1 spent.

climax.text: Communities that invest in all four phases recover faster and suffer fewer losses.

punchline.text: An ounce of mitigation beats a ton of response. Every single time.

quiz.question: Which phase of the disaster cycle is most cost-effective?
